description_long: This apartment building features a modern, multi-winged structure with three main high-rise sections connected at angles, each with a uniform grid of blue-tinted windows. The facade is predominantly white with subtle vertical ribbing, giving it a clean, contemporary appearance. A prominent feature is the large, red-tiled gabled roof section attached to one side, likely housing communal or commercial spaces. The windows are arranged in consistent horizontal rows, contributing to the building's geometric symmetry. The overall design suggests a high-density residential complex, typical of urban environments, with no visible balconies but ample window space for natural light. The building's mass and scale indicate reinforced concrete construction, suitable for accommodating many residents.
description: A large, modern apartment complex with three white high-rise wings, blue-tinted windows in a grid pattern, and a distinctive red-tiled gabled roof section.
description_view_0: The front view shows the three main high-rise wings with blue windows and the edge of the red-tiled roof section partially visible behind.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the red-tiled gabled roof section attached to the main building, with the high-rise wings receding into the background.
description_view_2: The rear view emphasizes the full expanse of the red-tiled roof and the back faces of the high-rise wings, with window grids clearly visible.
description_view_3: A side view focusing on the vertical ribbed facade of the tallest wing and the triangular outline of the red-tiled roof section.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in evenly spaced, horizontal rows across all visible facades, creating a regular and symmetrical grid pattern.
